steps.title: Locate the stated thesis
steps.content: The passage explicitly identifies the cause of Morales's obscurity: "One reason for Morales's relative obscurity is the enormous popularity of some of her peers"—namely Navarro and Ruiz, who "captivated the interest of US audiences" so much that Morales gets "overlooked."

steps.title: Define what the main idea must contain
steps.content: Any correct main-idea answer must capture two elements: (1) Morales is not well-known in the US, and (2) the cause is Navarro and Ruiz drawing public attention away from her. Anything that adds new facts, reverses the relationship, or substitutes a different cause is wrong.

steps.title: Eliminate by misread/overreach
steps.content:
• Choice A — Morales's work is not as well-known in the United States as it should be because Navarro and Ruiz draw so much of the public's attention.: directly restates both required elements. ✓
  • Choice B — During Morales's lifetime, her paintings were displayed in galleries in the United States much more frequently than paintings by Navarro and Ruiz were.: reverses the relationship and invents gallery-display data the passage never mentions. ✗
  • Choice C — Morales painted some of the same subjects that Navarro and Ruiz painted but used different techniques than they used.: overreach—the passage says nothing about shared subjects or technique differences. ✗
  • Choice D — Few of Morales's works are in galleries today because she produced only a small number of paintings.: substitutes a different cause (low output) that the passage never claims; the passage attributes obscurity to peers' popularity, not scarcity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
